CVE-2016-10169.title: The read_code function in read_words.c in Wavpack before 5.1.0 al ...
CVE-2016-10169.description: The read_code function in read_words.c in Wavpack before 5.1.0 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (out-of-bounds read) via a crafted WV file.

CVE-2018-10536.title: An issue was discovered in WavPack 5.1.0 and earlier. The WAV par ...
CVE-2018-10536.description: An issue was discovered in WavPack 5.1.0 and earlier. The WAV parser component contains a vulnerability that allows writing to memory because ParseRiffHeaderConfig in riff.c does not reject multiple format chunks.

CVE-2018-10538.title: An issue was discovered in WavPack 5.1.0 and earlier for WAV inpu ...
CVE-2018-10538.description: An issue was discovered in WavPack 5.1.0 and earlier for WAV input. Out-of-bounds writes can occur because ParseRiffHeaderConfig in riff.c does not validate the sizes of unknown chunks before attempting memory allocation, related to a lack of integer-overflow protection within a bytes_to_copy calculation and subsequent malloc call, leading to insufficient memory allocation.

CVE-2018-19840.title: The function WavpackPackInit in pack_utils.c in libwavpack.a in W ...
CVE-2018-19840.description: The function WavpackPackInit in pack_utils.c in libwavpack.a in WavPack through 5.1.0 allows attackers to cause a denial-of-service (resource exhaustion caused by an infinite loop) via a crafted wav audio file because WavpackSetConfiguration64 mishandles a sample rate of zero.

CVE-2020-35738.title: WavPack 5.3.0 has an out-of-bounds write in WavpackPackSamples in ...
CVE-2020-35738.description: WavPack 5.3.0 has an out-of-bounds write in WavpackPackSamples in pack_utils.c because of an integer overflow in a malloc argument. NOTE: some third-parties claim that there are later "unofficial" releases through 5.3.2, which are also affected.

CVE-2021-44269.title: An out of bounds read was found in Wavpack 5.4.0 in processing *. ...
CVE-2021-44269.description: An out of bounds read was found in Wavpack 5.4.0 in processing *.WAV files. This issue triggered in function WavpackPackSamples of file src/pack_utils.c, tainted variable cnt is too large, that makes pointer sptr read beyond heap bound.
